:::: {.panel-tabset .nav-pills} ## Prison Population
::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

``` ::: ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

Admission Types

Prison populations are shaped by two primary groups: new court commitments, or individuals newly incarcerated for a criminal offense, and parole returns and revocations, those who return to prison for failing to meet the conditions of their parole. In Pennsylvania in 2020, people in prison for new court commitments accounted for 76% of the prison population, while parole returns and revocations accounted for 24%.


``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

Who's in Prison?

``` ::: ::: ::: {.panel-tabset} ### Race and Ethinicity ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

In 2020, Black, non-Hispanic individuals made up the largest portion of people in prison for both new court commitments and parole returns and revocations.

``` ::: ::: ### Gender ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

In 2020, male individuals made up the largest portion of people in prison for both new court commitments and parole returns and revocations.

``` ::: ::: ### Age ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

In 2020, individuals aged 25-34 years made up the largest portion of people in prison for new court commitments, while individuals aged 35-44 years made up the largest portion of people in prison for parole returns and revocations.

``` ::: ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

## Parole Eligibility
::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

How is Parole Eligibility Determined?

When imposing a sentence of partial or total confinement, the court must impose a minimum and a maximum sentence. The court has discretion to set any maximum sentence within the statutory range, but the minimum term may not be greater than one-half the maximum selected by the court. Minimum sentences can be shorter than onehalf of the maximum term, in the court’s discretion, and can be as short as one day. Inmates in state correctional facilities are eligible for parole release after the expiration of their minimum sentences.14 In practice, inmates are automatically scheduled for their parole hearing before their minimum eligibility date. The Board may not grant parole to inmates condemned to death, or sentenced to life imprisonment. At the time of sentencing, the court is required to aggregate all consecutive sentences and to indicate the aggregate minimum term to be served, which is not to exceed one half of the maximum aggregate term.


``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

Parole-Eligible Prison Population

In 2020, there were 5,941 people were eligible for parole but still in prison for new court commitments and with original sentence lengths ranging from 1 to 25 years. This group made up 15% of the prison population.



``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

People who are Eligible for Parole

``` ::: ::: ::: {.panel-tabset} ### Race and Ethnicity ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

In 2020, White, non-Hispanic people made up the largest proportion of those eligible for parole yet still incarcerated, comprising 52% of the parole-eligible population serving time for new court commitments and with an original sentence length between 1 to 25 years.


``` ::: ::: ### Gender ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

In 2020, male people made up the largest proportion of those eligible for parole yet still incarcerated, comprising 95% of the parole-eligible population serving time for new court commitments and with an original sentence length between 1 to 25 years.


``` ::: ::: ### Age ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

In 2020, people who were between the ages of 25-34 years made up the largest proportion of those eligible for parole yet still incarcerated, comprising 35% of the parole-eligible population serving time for new court commitments and with an original sentence length between 1 to 25 years.


``` ::: ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

Sentence Lengths

In 2020, the majority of the parole-eligible prison population serving sentences for new court commitments with sentence lengths ranging from 1 to 25 years were incarcerated with an original sentence length of 10 to 24.9 years, accounting for 41% of this group.



``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

Criminal Offenses

In 2020, the majority of the parole-eligible prison population serving sentences for new court commitments with sentence lengths ranging from 1 to 25 years were incarcerated for offenses related to rape or sexual assault, accounting for 26% of this group.


``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

Missing Data

This section will have information about missing parole eligibility data for the state once new NCRP data is released.
``` ::: :::
